headlines.title: Graham Platner considering ‘best path forward’ in Maine Senate race after denying sexual assault claim
headlines.description: Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner is denying a new allegation of sexual assault, an explosive development injecting tumult into a key Senate race.

headlines.title: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner denies new allegations of sexual assault
headlines.description: Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner is denying a new allegation of sexual assault, an explosive development injecting tumult into a race that is central to the Democratic Party’s path to winning back the Senate. Despite the denial, Platner said in a video that he is taking “time to reflect on the best path forward” with his candidacy.

headlines.title: Trump is trying to create a ‘Red Scare on steroids’: Presidential historian
headlines.description: Presidential historian Douglas Brinkley joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss the historical precedent of vilifying opponents and weighs in on the state of the U.S. presidency as America celebrates its 250th birthday.

headlines.title: Trump call with FIFA president over red card controversy could ‘put an asterisk’ on U.S. win
headlines.description: President Trump prepares to travel to a NATO summit in Turkey as he faces criticism for a phone call to FIFA’s president over a red card controversy involving U.S. star striker Folarin Balogun. NBC News correspondents Julie Tsirkin, Courtney Kube and former U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine Bill Taylor discuss how the NATO meeting could renew the spotlight on the war in Ukraine. NBC’s Kelly O'Donnell and Steve Patterson discuss the fallout of President Trump’s red card intervention.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Trump says he asked FIFA to review U.S. soccer star Folarin Balogun’s suspension
headlines.description: The striker’s red card prompted fury from American soccer fans and concerns about the team’s prospects without its star player.

headlines.title: Interior secretary says administration will use the same company to repair Reflecting Pool
headlines.description: The administration has faced scrutiny for awarding a multimillion-dollar, no-bid contract to a company that previously worked on Trump properties.

headlines.title: Mallory McMorrow ends bid for Democratic Senate nomination in Michigan
headlines.description: The state senator’s decision sets up a two-person primary between former public health official Abdul El-Sayed and Rep. Haley Stevens.

headlines.title: Paul Pelosi could face hit-and-run charges after hitting a parked car in California
headlines.description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi’s husband admitted to officers he’d hit something but wasn’t sure what it was, so kept driving, the sheriff’s office said.

headlines.title: The Dan Sullivan saga in the Alaska Senate race is under investigation, sources say
headlines.description: Dan J. Sullivan, a former teacher, is mounting an effort to unseat Sen. Dan S. Sullivan, who has been in office since 2015.

headlines.title: Trump warns of communist ‘threat’ and touts America’s strength at Mount Rushmore
headlines.description: No presidential distinction rivals having one’s likeness carved onto Mt. Rushmore — an honor destined to elude Donald Trump, though his allies have pushed for it.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.
